div.p: This case-sensitive code system https://dhis2.org/fhir/cancer-registry/CodeSystem/cr-observation-codes defines the following codes:

tr.td: ICD-O-3 topography code indicating the anatomical site of the primary tumour.
ICD-O-3 morphology code (4-digit histological type) of the neoplasm.
ICD-O-3 behaviour code of the neoplasm (0=benign, 1=uncertain, 2=in situ, 3=malignant).
How the cancer diagnosis was established. Coded according to the ICD-O-3 basis of diagnosis classification (codes 0–9).
Higher-level anatomical site grouping derived from the ICD-O-3 topography.
Date of incidence (diagnosis date) of the cancer case.
Age of the patient at the date of incidence.
Administrative address code of the patient at incidence.
Sequential tumor number within the patient's registration.
Follow-up vital status of the registered cancer patient (alive, dead, or unknown).
Date of death of the patient.
Type of documentation source that notified the cancer registry about a case (e.g. hospital, laboratory, death certificate).
Tumor number that this source notification relates to.

description.value: Code system defining observation type codes for cancer registry observations. Used where no standard LOINC code exists for cancer-registry-specific concepts.

definition.value: ICD-O-3 topography code indicating the anatomical site of the primary tumour.
ICD-O-3 morphology code (4-digit histological type) of the neoplasm.
ICD-O-3 behaviour code of the neoplasm (0=benign, 1=uncertain, 2=in situ, 3=malignant).
How the cancer diagnosis was established. Coded according to the ICD-O-3 basis of diagnosis classification (codes 0–9).
Higher-level anatomical site grouping derived from the ICD-O-3 topography.
Date of incidence (diagnosis date) of the cancer case.
Age of the patient at the date of incidence.
Administrative address code of the patient at incidence.
Sequential tumor number within the patient's registration.
Follow-up vital status of the registered cancer patient (alive, dead, or unknown).
Date of death of the patient.
Type of documentation source that notified the cancer registry about a case (e.g. hospital, laboratory, death certificate).
Tumor number that this source notification relates to.
